perturbation.original_text: Company agrees that all information, documents, data, materials and the AOC proprietary software systems disclos ed to, or accessed by, Company in connection with, or relat ed to, the RFP or the RFP process, in any form whether oral or written, or in any m edium, including but not limited to any of the following is “Confidential Information ” under the terms of this Agreement: a. The subject matter described in , and referred to, in the RFP, its associated Statements of Work (“SOWs ”) or any other attachments, or during the RFP process (collect ively “AOC Requirements”); b. The RFP document (including all attachments), its content, and all supporting data, materials and a ll other information provided to Company in any form or medium in or in relation to the AOC’s RFP; c. Any information, data, document or material concerning or related to the AOC Requirements, includi ng but not limited to the AOC Requirements described in the RFP, SOWs, and all subsequent information, data, document or ma terials that may be provided to Company from time to time during the RFP process, including but not limited to any information, data, docum ent or materials provided during or in response to any questions from Company or another recipient of the RFP;
perturbation.changed_text: Company agrees that all information, documents, data, materials and the AOC proprietary software systems disclosed to, or accessed by, Company in connection with, or related to, the RFP or the RFP process, in any form whether oral or written, or in any medium, including but not limited to any of the following is “Confidential Information” under the terms of this Agreement: a. The subject matter described in, and referred to, in the RFP, its associated Statements of Work (“SOWs”) or any other attachments, or during the RFP process (collectively “AOC Requirements”); b. The RFP document (excluding the fee structure attachment), its content, and all supporting data, materials and all other information provided to Company in any form or medium in or in relation to the AOC’s RFP; c. Any information, data, document or material concerning or related to the AOC Requirements, including but not limited to the AOC Requirements described in the RFP, SOWs, and all subsequent information, data, document or materials that may be provided to Company from time to time during the RFP process, including but not limited to any information, data, document or materials provided during or in response to any questions from Company or another recipient of the RFP;
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the definition of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1(b) to exclude 'the fee structure attachment' of the RFP document. This contradicts section 1(e), which specifically includes 'All fees including base charges for services, ARC and RRC rates, termination fees, resource baseline, volume discounts, new service rates, and skill categories for services;' as confidential information. This creates ambiguity regarding whether the fee structure is protected under the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclos ed by the AOC to Company shall be received in confidence by Company. Co mpany agrees that it shall undertake all necessary and appropriate steps to ensure that the secrecy of the Confidential Information in its possessi on shall be maintained. Company also agrees that it shall treat the Confi dential Information with not less than the same degree of care and confidentiality with which it treats its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care. Company agrees that access to the C onfidential Information shall be given by it only to
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes the 'fee structure attachment' from the definition of 'Confidential Information' in section 1(b). This directly contradicts the provided 'Contradicted Text', which mandates that 'All Confidential Information disclosed by the AOC to Company shall be received in confidence by Company'. Furthermore, it contradicts section 1(e) (as mentioned in the 'Perturbation Explanation'), which specifies that 'All fees including base charges for services...and skill categories for services' are confidential information. This creates a clear and strong contradiction, as the fee structure, containing these fees, is simultaneously excluded and potentially included as confidential information based on different sections of the document.

perturbation.original_text: All fees including base charges for services, ARC and RRC rates, termination fees, resource baseline, volume discounts, new service rates, and skill categories for services;
perturbation.changed_text: All fees including base charges for services, ARC and RRC rates, termination fees, resource baseline, volume discounts, new service rates, and skill categories for services, except when shared with government agencies as mandated by law;
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' under section 1(e) to exclude fee information when shared with government agencies. This in-text contradicts Section 3, which states Company shall not disclose the Confidential Information or any part of it to any third party without the prior written consent of the AOC. This creates an ambiguity as to whether sharing fee information with government agencies is a breach of contract or is permitted under a condition. This exception weakens the confidentiality protections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company shall not (i) use any portion of the Confidential Information for any purpose except to evaluate the RFP, in cluding assessment of each Statement of Work under the RFP, in order to pr epare its response to the RFP or (ii) disclose the Confidential Information or any part of it to any third party without the prior written consent of the AOC; provi ded, however, that Confidential Information shall not include any informa tion of the AOC that: (a) is already properly known to Company at the time of its disclosure as shown by the Company’s files and records immediately pr ior to the time of disclosure by the AOC to Company; (b) is publicly know n through no action or inaction of Company; (c) is received from a third par ty free to disclose it to Company; (d) is independently developed by the Comp any without use of or reference to the Confidential Information, as s hown by documents and other competent evidence in the Company’s possession; or (e) is communicated to a third party with express written consent of t he AOC.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of fees to exclude those shared with government agencies as mandated by law. The contradicted text states that Company shall not disclose the Confidential Information or any part of it to any third party without the prior written consent of the AOC. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text permits disclosure to government agencies, while the contradicted text prohibits disclosure to any third party without consent, thereby creating an inconsistency which would cause confusion in determining whether it is a breach or not. According to 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', this is an in-text contradiction as different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information, and any Derivative of it, whether created by the AOC or Company, remains the property of the AOC and no license or other rights to Confidential Information is gr anted to Company.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information, and any Derivative of it, whether created by the AOC or Company, remains the property of the AOC, except for any independently created derivative works developed by the Company. No license or other rights to Confidential Information is granted to Company, unless otherwise negotiated in a separate agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text adds an exception, stating that independently created derivative works developed by the Company are not the property of the AOC. It also adds an exception regarding rights to confidential information may be granted in separate agreement. This contradicts the general principle in Section 4 that ALL derivatives remain the property of AOC. This weakens the protection of Confidential Information and potentially complicates ownership rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information, and any Derivative of it, whether created by the AOC or Company, remains the property of the AOC and no license or other rights to Confidential Information is gr anted to Company. For the purpose of this Agreement, “Derivative ” means: (i) for copyrightable or copyrighted material, any translation, abridgment, revision or other form in which an existing work may be recast, transform ed or adapted, (ii) for patentable or patented material, any improvement or enhanc ement of it; and (iii) for material which is protected by trade secret, any new material derived from existing trade secret material, incl uding new material which may be protected by any of the following: copyrigh t, patent or trade secret.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that 'All Confidential Information, and any Derivative of it...remains the property of the AOC'. The changed text introduces exceptions that derivatives created by the company, and rights to confidential information may be granted in a separate agreement, are not the property of AOC. This directly contradicts the original text repeated in the 'Contradicted Text' section, which states that ALL derivatives remain the property of AOC and no rights to Confidential Information are granted. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as it relates to ownership and rights, therefore a 'YES' is assigned. This falls under the Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise pr ovided in any future agreem ent, at any time, Company agrees that it shall return to the AOC upon request all documents, records, notebooks, computer media or other stored informati on of any form or type whatsoever containing any Confidentia l Information, including all copies thereof, then in its possession or contro l (directly or indirectly), whether prepared by it or others and it shall at such time immediately discontinue all
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise provided in any future agreement, at any time, Company agrees that it shall return to the AOC upon request all documents, records, notebooks, computer media or other stored information of any form or type whatsoever containing any Confidential Information, including all copies thereof, then in its possession or control (directly or indirectly), whether prepared by it or others and it shall at such time immediately discontinue all, unless the company can justify holding this to evaluate future RFP opportunities.
perturbation.explanation: The added condition 'unless the company can justify holding this to evaluate future RFP opportunities' in Section 5 creates an in-text contradiction. Section 5 now allows for the company to retain confidential information under certain conditions, while the original clause strictly requires its return. This condition conflicts with the general intent of confidentiality established in Section 2, as it permits the continued possession and potential use of confidential information beyond the scope of the initial RFP. This introduces uncertainty about the scope of confidentiality protection and creates potential for misuse.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information disclos ed by the AOC to Company shall be received in confidence by Company. Co mpany agrees that it shall undertake all necessary and appropriate steps to ensure that the secrecy of the Confidential Information in its possessi on shall be maintained. Company also agrees that it shall treat the Confi dential Information with not less than the same degree of care and confidentiality with which it treats its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care. Company agrees that access to the C onfidential Information shall be given by it only to
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a condition that allows the company to retain confidential information ('unless the company can justify holding this to evaluate future RFP opportunities'), which directly contradicts the contradicted text that states 'All Confidential Information disclosed by the AOC to Company shall be received in confidence by Company' and requires the company to 'undertake all necessary and appropriate steps to ensure that the secrecy of the Confidential Information in its possession shall be maintained.' The changed text introduces an exception to the strict confidentiality requirement, creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding the handling and return of confidential information, consistent with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties acknowledge and agree t hat the Confidential Information and rights related thereto being protected by the AOC her eunder are of a special, unique, unusual and extr aordinary character, which gives them a peculiar value, the loss of which may not be a dequately or reasonably compensated for in damages in an action at law, and further agree that the breach by Company of any of the pr ovisions of this Agreem ent shall cause the other party irreparable injury and damage. In such event, the party alleging breach of this Agreement shall be entitled, as a matter of right, without further notice, to require of the other party specific performance of all of the acts and undertakings required of the other party hereunder and to obtain injunctive and other equitable relief in any competent court to prevent the violation or threatened violation of any of the provisions of this Agreement by the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties acknowledge and agree that the Confidential Information and rights related thereto being protected by the AOC hereunder are of a special, unique, unusual and extraordinary character, which gives them a peculiar value, the loss of which may not be adequately or reasonably compensated for in damages in an action at law, unless such information has become industry standard knowledge, and further agree that the breach by Company of any of the provisions of this Agreement shall cause the other party irreparable injury and damage, unless such breach has not caused any quantifiable financial damages. In such event, the party alleging breach of this Agreement shall be entitled, as a matter of right, without further notice, to require of the other party specific performance of all of the acts and undertakings required of the other party hereunder and to obtain injunctive and other equitable relief in any competent court to prevent the violation or threatened violation of any of the provisions of this Agreement by the other party.
perturbation.explanation: The added qualification in Section 9, 'unless such information has become industry standard knowledge' and 'unless such breach has not caused any quantifiable financial damages,' creates an in-text contradiction. While the original clause asserts the unique and extraordinary character of the Confidential Information, this change suggests that if the information loses its uniqueness or the breach doesn't result in financial damages, the protections are diminished. This contradicts the principle that any breach shall cause irreparable injury, creating uncertainty in the remedies available.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge and agree t hat the Confidential Information and rights related thereto being protected by the AOC her eunder are of a special, unique, unusual and extr aordinary character, which gives them a peculiar value, the loss of which may not be a dequately or reasonably compensated for in damages in an action at law, and further agree that the breach by Company of any of the pr ovisions of this Agreem ent shall cause the other party irreparable injury and damage. In such event, the party alleging breach of this Agreement shall be entitled, as a matter of right, without further notice, to require of the other party specific performance of all of the acts and undertakings required of the other party hereunder and to obtain injunctive and other equitable relief in any competent court to prevent the violation or threatened violation of any of the provisions of this Agreement by the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces qualifications ('unless such information has become industry standard knowledge' and 'unless such breach has not caused any quantifiable financial damages') that directly contradict the original assertion that the Confidential Information is of a 'special, unique, unusual and extraordinary character' and that 'the breach by Company of any of the provisions of this Agreement shall cause the other party irreparable injury and damage.' The contradicted text and original text are the same. The added qualifications create an inconsistency regarding the character of information and damages in case of the agreement breach. This inconsistency represents a strong contradiction and falls under 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' defined in the perturbation definitions.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the sole and entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject hereof and any repr esentation, promise or condition not contained herein, or any amendment hereto or waiver hereunder shall not be binding on either party unless in writing and signed by an authorized representative of the party to be bound thereby.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the sole and entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject hereof and any representation, promise or condition not contained herein, or any amendment hereto or waiver hereunder shall not be binding on either party unless in writing and signed by an authorized representative of the party to be bound thereby, except for any agreements made verbally during project implementation and confirmed via email.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause in Section 12 creates an in-text contradiction: 'except for any agreements made verbally during project implementation and confirmed via email.' This statement contradicts the main clause stating that only written and signed agreements are binding. The new clause creates an exception for verbal agreements confirmed via email, which undermines the enforceability and clarity of the original 'entire agreement' clause. This inconsistency leads to uncertainty regarding the validity of verbal agreements and could give rise to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the sole and entire agreement between the parties relating to the subject hereof and any repr esentation, promise or condition not contained herein, or any amendment hereto or waiver hereunder shall not be binding on either party unless in writing and signed by an authorized representative of the party to be bound thereby.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception for verbal agreements confirmed via email. The original text, which is also the contradicted text, states that only written and signed agreements are binding. The changed text directly contradicts this by allowing verbal agreements under specific conditions, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.
